perturbation.original_text: All such information furnished to the Company or its Representatives (as defined below) by or on behalf of MCI (irrespective of the form of communication and whether such information is furnished prior to, on or after the date hereof), and all analyses, compilations, data, studies, notes, interpretations, memoranda or other documents prepared by the Company or its Representatives containing or based in whole or in part on any such furnished information are collectively referred to herein as the "Confidential Information."
perturbation.changed_text: All such information furnished to the Company or its Representatives (as defined below) by or on behalf of MCI (irrespective of the form of communication and whether such information is furnished prior to, on or after the date hereof), and all analyses, compilations, data, studies, notes, interpretations, memoranda or other documents prepared by the Company or its Representatives containing or based in whole or in part on any such furnished information, **except for pricing data,** are collectively referred to herein as the "Confidential Information."
perturbation.explanation: By explicitly excluding 'pricing data' from the definition of 'Confidential Information' in this section, while other sections (specifically Section 2) refer to 'price' as part of the negotiations that should be kept confidential, we introduce an internal contradiction. This creates ambiguity around whether pricing discussions are protected under the confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as required by law, neither the Company nor its Representatives shall disclose to any person the existence, status or terms of any discussions, negotiations or agreements concerning a possible Transaction, including without limitation any offer, letter of intent, proposal, price, value or valuation, or any similar terms, agreements or understandings between the Company and MCI with respect thereto, or that the Company has received from MCI Confidential Information, without obtaining the prior written consent of the other, which consent will not be withheld unreasonably.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly excludes 'pricing data' from the definition of 'Confidential Information', while the contradicted text states that the 'price' of negotiations should not be disclosed. This creates a direct contradiction, as pricing information is now both excluded from and included within the confidential information definition. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: the Company shall (i) use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and for no other competitive or other purpose; (ii) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party, except for disclosures to its directors, officers, employees and representatives of its advisors (such as independent accountants, investment bankers and attorneys) acting on <PAGE> its behalf (such directors, officers, employees and representatives being referred to hereinafter collectively as its "Representatives") who in each case, in the Company's reasonable judgment, need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction; provided, however, that prospective financing sources shall not be considered "Representatives" to whom Confidential Information may be disclosed in accordance with this paragraph 1 unless specifically requested by the Company and consented to by MCI in writing
perturbation.changed_text: the Company shall (i) use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and for no other competitive or other purpose; (ii) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party, except for disclosures to its directors, officers, employees and representatives of its advisors (such as independent accountants, investment bankers and attorneys) acting on <PAGE> its behalf (such directors, officers, employees and representatives being referred to hereinafter collectively as its "Representatives") who in each case, in the Company's reasonable judgment, need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction; **however, prospective financing sources shall be considered "Representatives" to whom Confidential Information may be disclosed without needing explicit consent from MCI**; provided, however, that prospective financing sources shall not be considered "Representatives" to whom Confidential Information may be disclosed in accordance with this paragraph 1 unless specifically requested by the Company and consented to by MCI in writing
perturbation.explanation: By stating financing sources both can and can't be considered representatives without consent, we introduce a contradiction. The phrase "however, prospective financing sources shall be considered 'Representatives' to whom Confidential Information may be disclosed without needing explicit consent from MCI" directly contradicts the condition after the 'provided' clause. This creates uncertainty in enforcement, it causes in-text contradiction as you have conflicting clauses inside the same paragraph.
perturbation.contradicted_text: the Company shall (i) use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and for no other competitive or other purpose; (ii) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party, except for disclosures to its directors, officers, employees and representatives of its advisors (such as independent accountants, investment bankers and attorneys) acting on <PAGE> its behalf (such directors, officers, employees and representatives being referred to hereinafter collectively as its "Representatives") who in each case, in the Company's reasonable judgment, need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction; however, prospective financing sources shall be considered "Representatives" to whom Confidential Information may be disclosed without needing explicit consent from MCI; provided, however, that prospective financing sources shall not be considered "Representatives" to whom Confidential Information may be disclosed in accordance with this paragraph 1 unless specifically requested by the Company and consented to by MCI in writing
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states that prospective financing sources are not considered 'Representatives' unless specifically requested and consented to by MCI in writing. The changed text includes the phrase 'however, prospective financing sources shall be considered 'Representatives' to whom Confidential Information may be disclosed without needing explicit consent from MCI'. This directly contradicts the condition after the 'provided' clause, creating an in-text contradiction within the same paragraph, as the contradicted text is the original text itself after the change. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' as it creates uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: All written Confidential Information delivered by or on behalf of MCI to the Company pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of MCI and upon the written request of MCI, the Company shall (i) promptly return such Confidential Information and shall not retain any copies or other reproductions or extracts thereof, (ii) destroy or have destroyed all memoranda, notes, reports, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations, or other documents derived from or containing Confidential Information, and all copies and other reproductions and extracts thereof, and (iii) upon written request by MCI provide a certificate to MCI certifying that the foregoing <PAGE> materials have, in fact, been destroyed or returned, signed by an authorized officer supervising such destruction or return.
perturbation.changed_text: All written Confidential Information delivered by or on behalf of MCI to the Company pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of MCI and upon the written request of MCI, the Company shall (i) promptly return such Confidential Information and shall not retain any copies or other reproductions or extracts thereof, (ii) destroy or have destroyed all memoranda, notes, reports, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations, or other documents derived from or containing Confidential Information, and all copies and other reproductions and extracts thereof, and (iii) upon written request by MCI provide a certificate to MCI certifying that the foregoing <PAGE> materials have, in fact, been destroyed or returned, signed by an authorized officer supervising such destruction or return. **However, the Company may retain one copy of all Confidential Information for archival purposes.**
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'However, the Company may retain one copy of all Confidential Information for archival purposes' directly contradicts the initial statement 'shall not retain any copies or other reproductions or extracts thereof'. This creates an ambiguity and in-text contradiction regarding the obligation to completely return or destroy all confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All written Confidential Information delivered by or on behalf of MCI to the Company pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of MCI and upon the written request of MCI, the Company shall (i) promptly return such Confidential Information and shall not retain any copies or other reproductions or extracts thereof, (ii) destroy or have destroyed all memoranda, notes, reports, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations, or other documents derived from or containing Confidential Information, and all copies and other reproductions and extracts thereof, and (iii) upon written request by MCI provide a certificate to MCI certifying that the foregoing <PAGE> materials have, in fact, been destroyed or returned, signed by an authorized officer supervising such destruction or return. However, the Company may retain one copy of all Confidential Information for archival purposes.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'However, the Company may retain one copy of all Confidential Information for archival purposes.' to the original text, which directly contradicts the preceding clause 'shall not retain any copies or other reproductions or extracts thereof.' This creates a direct conflict because the company is initially instructed to return all confidential information without keeping any copies, but is then allowed to retain one copy. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text because the change happens within the same section, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The term "Confidential Information" does not include information that (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the Company or its Representatives in violation of this Agreement; or (ii) was or becomes available to the Company from a source other than MCI or its Representatives, provided that such source is not known by the Company to be bound by an obligation of confidentiality to MCI or its Representatives or (iii) was already known to the Company at the time of its disclosure by MCI.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Confidential Information" does not include information that (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the Company or its Representatives in violation of this Agreement; or (ii) was or becomes available to the Company from a source other than MCI or its Representatives, **regardless** of whether such source is known by the Company to be bound by an obligation of confidentiality to MCI or its Representatives or (iii) was already known to the Company at the time of its disclosure by MCI.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'provided that such source is not known by the Company to be bound by an obligation of confidentiality to MCI or its Representatives' to 'regardless of whether such source is known by the Company to be bound by an obligation of confidentiality to MCI or its Representatives', we create an ambiguity. This means that information obtained from a source with confidentiality obligations is now NOT considered confidential, which directly contradicts the spirit and intent of the entire agreement. It is an in-text contradiction because the purpose of the document is being affected negatively.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As a condition to being furnished the Confidential Information, the Company agrees as follows: 1. NON-DISCLOSURE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. (a) the Company shall (i) use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and for no other competitive or other purpose; (ii) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party, except for disclosures to its directors, officers, employees and representatives of its advisors (such as independent accountants, investment bankers and attorneys) acting on <PAGE> its behalf (such directors, officers, employees and representatives being referred to hereinafter collectively as its "Representatives") who in each case, in the Company's reasonable judgment, need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction; provided, however, that prospective financing sources shall not be considered "Representatives" to whom Confidential Information may be disclosed in accordance with this paragraph 1 unless specifically requested by the Company and consented to by MCI in writing; (iii) inform its Representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and direct its Representatives to treat the Confidential Information confidentially; (iv) take all additional reasonable precautions necessary to prevent the disclosure of the Confidential Information by its Representatives to any third party; and (v) be responsible for any breach of this Agreement (the "Agreement") by its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that information from a source with confidentiality obligations IS considered confidential information. The changed text explicitly states that information from a source with confidentiality obligations is NOT considered confidential information ('regardless of whether such source is known by the Company to be bound by an obligation of confidentiality to MCI or its Representatives'). This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Company must not disclose Confidential Information. If information from a source with confidentiality obligations is no longer considered confidential, then the company is free to disclose it, completely undermining the purpose of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: The Company agrees that, for a period of two years from the date of this Agreement, it will not initiate or maintain contact (except in the ordinary course of business) with any director, officer, employee, distributor or customer of MCI regarding MCI's business operations, prospects or finances, except as may be permitted by the Contact Persons for due diligence purposes. It is expressly understood that this Agreement is not intended to limit the right of the parties to compete with one another in the ordinary course.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company agrees that, for a period of two years from the date of this Agreement, it will not initiate or maintain contact (except in the ordinary course of business) with any director, officer, employee, distributor or customer of MCI regarding MCI's business operations, prospects or finances, **even if** permitted by the Contact Persons for due diligence purposes. It is expressly understood that this Agreement is not intended to limit the right of the parties to compete with one another in the ordinary course.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'except as may be permitted by the Contact Persons for due diligence purposes' to 'even if permitted by the Contact Persons for due diligence purposes', a direct contradiction is introduced. It first says contact is prohibited, then carves out an exception, and then removes the exception. The in-text contradiction lies within the paragraph itself, making enforcement unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company agrees that, for a period of two years from the date of this Agreement, it will not initiate or maintain contact (except in the ordinary course of business) with any director, officer, employee, distributor or customer of MCI regarding MCI's business operations, prospects or finances, even if permitted by the Contact Persons for due diligence purposes. It is expressly understood that this Agreement is not intended to limit the right of the parties to compete with one another in the ordinary course.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text to remove an exception ('except as may be permitted by the Contact Persons for due diligence purposes') and replace it with an explicit prohibition ('even if permitted by the Contact Persons for due diligence purposes'). This creates a direct contradiction within the same paragraph because it first implies that contact might be allowed under certain conditions (due diligence), then immediately states that such contact is not allowed under any circumstances, creating an in-text contradiction as described by the explanation. The changed text and contradicted text are the same because the contradiction exists within the same clause of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: It is understood and agreed that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by the Company and that MCI shall be entitled to equitable relief, including specific performance and injunction, as a remedy for any such <PAGE> breach or threatened breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement by the Company but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity to MCI, including remedies pursuant to applicable laws relating to trade secrets.
perturbation.changed_text: It is understood and agreed that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by the Company and that MCI shall be entitled to equitable relief, including specific performance and injunction, as a remedy for any such <PAGE> breach or threatened breach. Such remedies **shall** be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement by the Company but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity to MCI, including remedies pursuant to applicable laws relating to trade secrets.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies' to 'shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies' introduces a direct in-text contradiction within the same sentence. It first states that equitable relief is *not* the only remedy, and then immediately states that it *is* the only remedy. This creates massive ambiguity regarding the available recourse for MCI in the event of a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is understood and agreed that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by the Company and that MCI shall be entitled to equitable relief, including specific performance and injunction, as a remedy for any such <PAGE> breach or threatened breach. Such remedies shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement by the Company but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity to MCI, including remedies pursuant to applicable laws relating to trade secrets.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself and therefore creates a contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text', because the 'Contradicted Text' and 'Changed Text' are the same, meaning that the contradiction is self-evident, and therefore exists.
